vulnerability.reference: Physicians over-rely on overconfident AI diagnostic suggestions, neglecting contradictory clinical evidence.
vulnerability.generated: Radiologists rely on definitive-appearing AI outputs without access to calibration signals, preventing detection of overconfident incorrect pattern matches.

impact.reference: Increased misdiagnosis rates, patient harm, and erosion of trust in diagnostic AI.
impact.generated: Reinforced incorrect pattern associations cause systematic diagnostic errors, wrong treatments, and patient harm at population scale.

dose_response.reference: Overconfident sparse Hopfield retrievals cause radiologists to over-rely on AI suggestions, leading to increased misdiagnosis rates and patient harm.
dose_response.generated: Sparse Hopfield-Fenchel-Young networks producing overconfident, miscalibrated retrievals under shifts, through radiologists using AI diagnostic tools, trigger reliance on definitive-appearing outputs without calibration, preventing detection of overconfident incorrect matches, leading to reinforced incorrect associations, diagnostic errors, wrong treatments, and patient harm.
